behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between taxonomy configuration, request construction, and API client payload wiring. A pure unit test of prompt-building logic can miss dropped/renamed fields at module boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real classifier module to a test double API client (or local HTTP stub) that captures outbound payloads. Call classify() and assert the transmitted label list exactly matches configured taxonomy values (no missing/extra labels).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily local validation/normalization logic: given an out-of-taxonomy label, map to "unknown". It does not require real I/O to expose the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the response-normalization/classification decision path with an invalid label fixture (e.g., "medical") and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output stability. Mocked or deterministic stubs would hide true nondeterminism from the external AI API, which is the real failure mode here.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real API classifications for the same document under production model/settings and assert label consistency across runs (with a predefined pass criterion, e.g., 100/100 identical or bounded variance policy).

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Real token consumption depends on provider-side tokenization/accounting and full request shape. Mocks can under-report or misrepresent actual usage, masking real over-budget failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a real API call with a representative document and actual prompt/template, then assert reported token usage (or provider billing metadata) is <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in retry-policy wiring across client, error translation, and backoff logic. This is best caught with modules wired together; real external API is not strictly required if rate-limit responses are faithfully simulated.
behaviors.test_description: Use a controllable API stub that returns rate-limit errors first (e.g., 429) and success on a later attempt. Assert the classifier waits/backs off, retries, and finally returns the successful label instead of surfacing the initial error.
